attacking_style: Opportunistic finisher who exploits tactical hesitation in small group scenarios, launching decisive attacks inside final 2km on uphill finishes to maximize climbing advantage.
team_tactics: Team operates with flexible GC assignment based on race situation rather than pre-determined leadership hierarchy, allowing improvisation when primary plans fail.
terrain_preference: Uphill finishes where climbing ability becomes decisive in reduced groups; thrives in breakaway scenarios requiring both endurance and explosive finishing power.
vulnerability: Insufficient data to determine consistent vulnerabilities; reliance on breakaway success suggests potential weakness in pure GC mountain stages or large sprint finishes.
key_patterns: Wins from small breakaway groups (8 riders) rather than large peloton scenarios.
Attacks with under 2km remaining on climbing finishes to leverage power advantage.
Receives GC opportunities through team improvisation rather than pre-race designation.
form_trajectory: unknown: Single race observation insufficient to establish trajectory despite positive result and GC freedom signal
